results.title: sisyphus-coverage-c04-reverse-elembreak
results.prompt: You are given the establishing WIDE shot of a scene plus two crops showing the litho mark-making. PRESERVE the world, character, and litho style. Render the SAME scene as a NEW full-frame shot — a high REVERSE angle, camera at the TOP of the broken escalator looking steeply DOWN its length to the hall below. Build the shot from these elements: (1) the two escalator balustrades and litter-strewn steps descend steeply away from camera, filling lower-center, converging toward the floor; (2) at the bottom, small, the stooped old man in the heavy dark overcoat hunched over his stalled overloaded shopping cart, seen from above and behind; (3) the BROKEN — OUT OF ORDER sign on its post near the foot of the escalator; (4) the derelict tiled transit hall floor beyond him with cracked tiles and scattered debris; (5) dim arched openings and grimy tiled walls; (6) a single muted terracotta-red spot in the gloom. Vertiginous, empty, lonely.

resolved_params.system_prompt: A late-19th / early-20th-century multi-color transfer lithograph on grained limestone. Forms drawn with a No. 2–3 tusche litho-crayon, its line greasy, blunt, dragging and crumbling on coarse stone — soft feathered edges, no fine detail, no clean vector edges, no anti-aliasing. Mark-making is pressure-differentiated: heavy dragging pressure on foreground darks so the stone grain breaks the ink into dense speckle (never a flat solid fill), light rapid gestural strokes elsewhere; all tone built from crayon pressure over grain. Three flat stencil-like color blocks — dusty rose-pink tint, a single muted terracotta-red spot, then the deep Prussian blue-black key plate — loose registration, unprinted warm-cream paper borders. Coarse paper tooth; visible ink starvation — speckled cream grain breaking through every dark area. Perspective loose and hand-drawn. Restricted tri-tone palette: warm cream, dusty rose-pink, deep Prussian blue, one muted terracotta-red accent. Stark flat high-contrast graphic shapes. No gradients, no glow, no photorealism, no flat opaque digital fills, never a sterile white background.
